'''Jason Chan''' (born 26 January 1984) is a former [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Celtic Crusaders]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]],  primarily as a {{rlp|sr}}, but also as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|pr}}.

==Background==
He was born in Sydney, Australia.

He is of Chinese descent.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=45759 |title=China set for Rugby League - rleague.com |accessdate=2 October 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121014022307/http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=45759 |archivedate=14 October 2012  }}</ref>

==Early career==
Chan played junior football for the [[Windsor Wolves]] in the [[Jim Beam Cup]].  His 16 tries and 64 goals helped the Wolves to win the 2008 title.<ref name="Jason Chan player profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.crusadersrfl.com/teammember.php?id=146 |title=Jason Chan player profile |publisher=Crusaders RL |accessdate=3 May 2010 |url-status=usurped |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101121125445/http://crusadersrfl.com/teammember.php?id=146 |archivedate=21 November 2010 }}</ref>

==Super League career==
Chan joined the Crusaders in 2009 as they embarked on their first [[Super League]] season. Chan played 23 games for Crusaders that season, including all three of their wins.  In 2010, Chan maintained his position in first team, earning the number 12 jumper. 
Following the demise of the Crusaders, Chan joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]], starting in 2012.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40642 Mellars makes Wildcats move] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111014041043/http://rleague.com/content/article.php?id=40642 |date=14 October 2011 }} ''rleague.com'', 1 August 2011</ref>

Chan spent part of the 2014 season on loan at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. He returned to Australia at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League: Jason Chan to return to Australia at the end of the season|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9430887/super-league-jason-chan-to-return-to-australia-at-the-end-of-the-season|website=Sky Sports|accessdate=18 January 2015|date=20 August 2014}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In the lead up to the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], Chan was named to the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team#Current squad|Papua New Guinea training squad]]<ref name="PNG name preliminary squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx |title=PNG name preliminary squad |publisher=RLWC.com |date=1 August 2008 |accessdate=6 August 2008 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100410150913/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx |archivedate=10 April 2010 }}</ref> and subsequently was named to the final World Cup squad.<ref name="Wilshere handed Kumuls captaincy">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7669326.stm|title=Wilshere handed Kumuls captaincy|publisher=BBC|date=14 October 2008|access-date=14 October 2008}}</ref> He played in all three of Papua New Guinea's matches during the World Cup, scoring a single try for the Kumuls in their loss against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref name="Rugby League World Cup 2008 Results">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/results/|title=Rugby League World Cup 2008 Results|publisher=RLWC.com|accessdate=15 July 2009}}{{dead link|date=November 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Chan was named as part of the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] squad for the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].<ref>[http://www.thenational.com.pg/?q=node/1554 Kumuls named] ''The National'', 12 October 2009</ref> Chan played in both games for PNG, scoring a try in the first round against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].

He played for [[Papua New Guinea rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].
